summary.intent: Fix controlled-state event bugs in the navigation menu component of ars-ui (PR #691), specifically addressing duplicate ValueChange callbacks, missing close requests, and stale open-key registration issues.
summary.outcome: All three controlled-rendered event threads were resolved with fixes in commit ae06d4b2, and a follow-up commit fixed a stale-key guard in open_item_plan. CI passed and changes were pushed.

repo: Navigation menu uses a controlled/rendered distinction where internal state can lag behind controlled state — event logic must use the 'effective rendered item' not raw internal state.
open_item_plan is the central path for direct open, pointer-enter, and timer-fired open actions — guards placed there cover all three.
Codex review is triggered by posting '@codex review' as a PR comment via gh CLI.
The repo uses cargo llvm-cov for coverage and cargo +nightly fmt for formatting.

code.finding: Open action must no-op against the rendered/effective open item to prevent duplicate ValueChange callbacks when controlled state changes are rejected.
SelectLink must follow rendered/effective open item (not internal state) so controlled menus still emit close requests when internal state is already Idle.
Pointer-leave and content-leave handlers must schedule CloseDelay based on the effective rendered item, not just internal state, to handle controlled state-lag cases.
open_item_plan needs a registration guard so it cannot request an item that is no longer registered, preventing stale-key panics/bugs across all open paths.

workflow: PR review threads can be resolved via GitHub GraphQL API mutation resolveReviewThread.
Replies to individual PR review comments are posted via gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pr}/comments/{comment_id}/replies.
CI polling with sleep+gh pr checks is used to wait for unit test runs to complete before acting on Codex review output.
Commit messages for AI-assisted fixes include 'Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>' attribution.

friction: CI polling required repeated manual sleep intervals (180s, 240s, 300s) to wait for Unit Tests to complete, with no automated notification.
Codex review surfaced an additional bug (stale-key guard) only after the initial three fixes were already committed and pushed, requiring a second fix cycle.
open_items: Verify that the stale-key registration guard in open_item_plan is sufficient or if similar guards are needed elsewhere in the navigation menu state machine.
Monitor final CI run for the stale-key fix commit to confirm all checks pass before merging PR #691.
